content.text:
IT Project Management
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UET2  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
Understand the principles and methods of IT project management
Execute the different phases of an IT project, from design to closure
Acquire skills in planning, organising, monitoring and controlling projects
Identify and manage risks related to IT projects
Use IT project management tools and techniques
Work in a team in a project management context
Recommended prior knowledge:
Software engineering, information systems.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
IT Project Management: Review
Definition and challenges of IT project management
IT project production process: realisation, management, quality processes
Different types of IT projects (software development, infrastructure, etc.)
Actors involved in an IT project
Traditional methodologies (V-model); agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban)
Planning and Estimation
Defining project scope and objectives
Project breakdown into tasks (WBS)
Schedule establishment (Gantt chart, PERT)
Resource estimation (time, budget, personnel, costs, COCOMO)
Planning tools (Microsoft Project, Asana, etc.)
Risk Management and Monitoring
Risk identification and analysis (SWOT matrix, Ishikawa diagram)
Risk probability and impact assessment
Project progress monitoring
Change management; communication and reporting
KPI dashboards and key performance metrics
Resource Management
Identification of required resources
Resource allocation to different tasks
Conflict and priority management
Quality Management
Defining quality criteria
Implementing quality control procedures
Verification and validation testing
Project Closure
Project closure documentation
Knowledge and skills transfer
Stakeholder satisfaction evaluation
Project review and lessons learned
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Project Management Institute (PMI) (2021).
A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK Guide)
.
Andrew Stellman, Jennifer Greene (2005).
Applied Software Project Management
. Theory In Practice.
Kerzner, Harold (2017).
Project Management: A Systems Approach to Planning, Scheduling, and Controlling
. Wiley.
R. S. Pressman, B. R. Maxim (2014).
Software Engineering: A Practitioner's Approach
. McGraw-Hill.
C. Aubry (2022).
Scrum un outil convivial pour une agilité radicale
. Editions Dunod.
